reviews.review_description:
It doesn't work. I cannot access any location. Disaster!

P.S.

Thank you very much for your reply. It does work by touching menu by controllers. 
I can access it. Excellent sceneries! Thanks 👍

developer_response.body:
Hi, can you email support at info@blueplanetvr.com, please state Quest model, OS version, everything checks out fine here. Meta's installer is known to be buggy, perhaps try another clean install from strong wireless.. 

UPDATE: looks like v83 disabled scene launch by beam, just reach out and touch the spheres to load-

reviews.review_description: Excellent way to explore! Lots of content. Can move around in each area and look all around. Even better when paired with Quest Game Optimizer.

Incredible. I wish I could give this more than 5 stars. It's that good. Everything works perfectly. Stunning. Spectacular. Mind blowing. I got the expanded edition. Worth it. I had hesitations. Finally got it. Exceeded expectations. All worries gone.
Brink VR has an added virtual environment for meta Quest. It is Horseshoe Bend River. Would Blue Planet VR ever include a virtual environment for meta Quest? I imagine that it would be a pretty good one if that ever happens.

developer_response.body: Great to hear, glad you love the locations! More to come!

reviews.review_description:
Purchased with the expansion pack bundle.  Base app works great and really enjoying the experience especially hang gliding! Much fun!!
Loaded fully(all 10) the first expansion pack but it still says touch to download.  The spheres sometimes don’t work or there is a delay when I touch.

Biggest problem is the other expansion packs won’t load.  I have plenty of space available.  When I try to touch for download my hand just goes through, no beep or other feedback that there is a problem of some kind.
Hope you can reply and give some guidance.  Currently stuck!
I did try rebooting headset, no help. 
Did not uninstall yet.  

7/9 update
Problems fixed with uninstall and reinstall.  Be careful of WiFi connection drops during download.  All good now!
Amazing VR App!!

Thanks!
Ken
I really wanted to like this app. The locations are pretty decent. However the quality of photogrammetry content is poor. Still not as bad as the interaction navigation and bugs in the app.

developer_response.body: Shiblee, can you specify what bugs you experienced-

reviews.review_description: I would highly recommend for people like me who wish to see all the wonders of the world even if just from the living room.  I’m thankful the developers brought those sites to me.  Love the hang gliding.   Beautifully done.  It would be nice if they enable friends with the app. to share the sights with me like Brink.

developer_response.body: Thank you Aloha! If you are from HI we have something special brewing for you!

reviews.review_description: Amazing meditative inspiring relaxing and simply breathtaking from flying over the canyons to ringing the historic bells to nature at its finest experience. Interactivity brings us the closest we can to physical traveling in the convenience of your home. Full of historic and scientific facts can be amazing learning tool for world cultural settings. Excellent music choices. BluePlanet VR is a must have for relaxation makes you feel hugged and inspired by nature’s grand beauty. I wonder where will it take me today! And just to think it’s only the beginning….

developer_response.body: Thank you for your enthusiasm! So much more to come!

reviews.review_description: This title would get 4 or even 5 stars if you could keep the ambient sounds and switch off the music.

developer_response.body: No worries George, it is on it's way.

reviews.review_description: You awkwardly move from marker to marker looking at generic stagnant pictures. It's like a less impressive Google maps. I want my money back

developer_response.body: Sorry you find no joy in it, but yes you are absolutely eligible for a refund if you used the app for less than 2 hours or use or within 14 days after purchase. From the Meta phone app, tap Menu then tap Settings, tap Purchase History, then select the app then tap Request Refund.

reviews.review_title: Love this app! Please keep developing :)
reviews.review_description:
So I really need to write reviews for all of the apps that we use… to cover the hood, the bad, and the ugly, but here I want to start because I would love to see future support for this app, and have loved what I have seen so far. 

So first, let us explore some of the wonderful aspects of Blue Planet:

After nearly 2 years of separation (thanks Covid) my mother was able to come visit us for the first time. On her arrival, I asked… would she like to try VR? After some brief trepidation about “not being good at video games” my mother put on the headset and was honestly blown away. Starting out with “First Steps”, I knew I needed to show her something amazing, something that would show her just how much it could allow her to do things she hadn’t done before, so visiting places she’d never seen seemed to be an excellent place to start.

And you know what? My mother actually started crying as she viewed places she’d always wanted to travel to but hadn’t yet had the opportunity to do so. Walking around in these spaces thoroughly transported her, allowing her to experience them in a way that she hadn’t been able to do for YEARS. Whether it was wandering through Carlsbad Caverns or soaring out over Icelandic glaciers, she stood and sat thoroughly enraptured by it all.

I’ve loved seeing these spaces too for the first time, and this app has added a number of distinctions to my bucket list.

Could the UI be bolstered by a few tweaks to improve the intuitiveness? Sure. Having the Intro video pop up directly in front of you when you first enter the app even could be great in helping people to become acquainted with it, but it’s still brilliant at doing what it does, and the photogrammetry is simply amazing.

I have also encountered some low poly textures at the corners of some spaces, but for me it hasn’t detracted at all from the experience, merely guided me back to where I can gain the best views.

One final note on suggestions: it would be wonderful to have a “camera” options for taking screenshots to share (with avatar integration for selfies potentially?) It could be great (when pinged with a tiny watermark) for sharing socially and allowing for others to discover just how amazing this app is!

Thank you so much for such a wonderful app, and I do hope that development continues and we can gain new places to explore in the future (I’m even game for some DLC packs if needed in order to help support additional content releases for more locations outside of the US.)

developer_response.body: Thank you for the good words. It is certainly not the app for everyone as these reviews show, but I am glad you understand the intention of the app, it's more about the experience of the locations than the UI polish or range of movement. Yes, I am busy capturing many more amazing locations and plan on adding DLC volumes, likely the first later this fall. Thanks again!

reviews.review_description:
BluePlanet-VR EXPLORE is a MUST HAVE for anyone who owns a Quest 2, if for no other reason than to show off the breathtaking potential of VR. Eric Hanson and his team of creatives have produced a masterpiece.

A retired college professor, teaching all levels of photography for more than thirty years, including alternative processes, and specializing in 3D nighttime, light-painted images, I would have been ecstatic to present my students with BluePlanet VR’s potential. 

EXPLORE offers a visual and experiential content that can best be described as mind-bending. Where else than VR can one perform a “face-plant” into the woody interior of a two-thousand-year-old bristlecone pine and, a fraction of a second later, soar like a red-tailed hawk over the frozen-lava remains of an Icelandic glacier? 

There’s a real kick in being able to Zen out in God-light illuminated Antelope Canyon, and when the brain shifts from alpha and delta waves towards mindful meditation, one’s heartbeat may even slow even to permit near-religious experience.

Imagine my excitement when I discovered a floating sphere hovering fifty or so feet out in the canyon beyond Fallen Roof Ruins. In this masterfully designed VR experience, you can leap onto that floating sphere and gaze back at the ruins from a position only attainable by drone. 

BPVR coaxes geology rockheads and ardent paleontologists to grab a spiral timetable and navigate their way up from Grand Canyon’s Vishnu schist in search of life forms more sexually active than slime molds. 

Smooth movements, vertigo minimal carpet rides, design architecture that would tantalize museum curators & students from any number of disciplines, and perhaps even sway constipated congressional legislators to consider alternative solutions to environmental and cultural preservation issues.  If there were a rating scale of 10, I would pop this app to a 20!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your enthusiasm and support, it helps us in future development efforts!

reviews.review_title: A treasure of immersive real-world sites
reviews.review_description: I've waited 40 years for an app like this to be possible in an untethered VR headset. Something like BlueplanetVR was a pipe dream when I was a grad student studying/developing 3D graphics at Cornell. What a joy to live long enough to experience this amazing collection of special places. The amount of expertise required, gear purchased, flights taken, helicopters chartered, permits approved, barriers temporarily removed, data gathered, processed, assembled, tested, and presented is simply mind-boggling. Were some compromises made to optimize Blueplanet VR for the Quest? Of course. Will twitch gamers lose patience with the meditative pace of this app? Probably. Is it incredible to be able to experience these locations immersively, many of which you'll likely never visit or even be allowed to enter? You bet. $25 for this app is a privilege... and a steal.

developer_response.body: Glad to hear your enthusiasm and support, thank you!

reviews.review_description: I was blown away by this app.  It is absolutely incredible!  I was shocked to come here and see the poor reviews as this app is like a portal to other worlds, and they are beautiful worlds.  The hang gliding is so relaxing, and so much fun!  I'm terrified of heights so was scared at first, but it actually feels amazing to fly and when I realized I couldn't fall I really loved the experience.  I've been in lockdown for what seems like ages and this let me explore places I couldn't visit in real life.  To be honest, it let me try things I'd never try in real life, such as hang gliding or visiting a mountain top monastery.  It's much more immersive than Wander, which I also really like.  This game reminds me a bit of Real VR Fishing, except instead of sitting in one spot, we can explore the area.  I had no issues with the game loading or freezing; it ran as smooth as silk  for me.  I can't wait until the pandemic is over so I can share this experience with my family and friends.

developer_response.body: Thanks Kat, I loved your enthusiasm in your youtube review. You are certainly our target audience!

reviews.review_description:
Excellent!
This is what I have been waiting for. Blue Planet Studios has succeeded in expanding the oculus platform into a truly rewarding experience. The worldly destinations chosen are exquisitely presented and provides the opportunity to visit and explore places few would ever get a chance to see.
Blueplanet VR Explore is a milestone in the development of VR content. I hope to see more titles that expands on this direction.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your enthusiasm and support!

reviews.review_description: I’m a big travel and history buff and I was really looking forward to this experience. Unfortunately it just doesn’t meet my expectations. I was hoping for a limited version of Google Earth VR which blows my mind every time I play it. This... is just always disappointing. Some locations look nice, but most are pretty uninteresting. There’s very limited movement, so calling them “walks” is a stretch. The hang gliding is ridiculous. The file size is crazy big, which would be excusable if it was fun. It’s also very buggy. I’m hoping it will get better in time but for now, I can’t recommend it.

developer_response.body: For now this is strictly an experiential work, but more guided narrative is in the works, there is much which can be done.

reviews.review_description: I am enjoying the view and experience at a leisurely pace.
Not Impressed! The most expensive game I've bought on here and it's by far the worst, and a waste of money.

developer_response.body: Sorry you find no joy in it, but you are absolutely eligible for a refund if you used the app for less than 2 hours or use or within 14 days after purchase. From the Meta phone app, tap Menu then tap Settings, tap Purchase History, then select the app then tap Request Refund.

reviews.review_title: BLUE PLANET: Fly like an Eagle!
reviews.review_description:
Blue Planet - MUST BUY! Cutting Edge!
Gregory Schaaf, Ph.D.
Retired Professor of Native American Studies,
University of California, California State University.
Fly like an Eagle to Native American Sites.
This is hang gliding over a glacier, walking through ruins, caves and seeing the most beautiful 4,700+ year old bristlecone pine tree. I loved this experience!

developer_response.body: Thank you Michelle! More in the works!

reviews.review_description: Won't allow you to transport anywhere I want a refund

developer_response.body: If you think it is defective, please contact us or yes, refund.
